messages.content: how does this project utilize code graph retrieval? is it essential? what if it was removed?

In this codebase, code graph retrieval is a first-class feature, not just an optimization.

It is used in three concrete ways:
- Inspect UI: structure browsing, symbol search, raw Cypher, and relationship lookup all go through `GraphService` and the `/structure`, `/query`, `/relationships`, and `/symbols` endpoints in [backend/app/main.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/main.py#L251) and [backend/app/services.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/services.py#L188). The frontend calls those directly in [frontend/lib/api.ts](/home/wniak/konceptura/frontend/lib/api.ts#L381) and [frontend/app/page.tsx](/home/wniak/konceptura/frontend/app/page.tsx#L661).
- Impact analysis: natural-language prompts are mapped to seed symbols, traversed over `CALLS` and `IMPORTS`, and turned into likely affected files in [backend/app/services.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/services.py#L323).
- Codex build loop: when `use_graph_context` is enabled, the app injects graph-derived hints like likely files and seed symbols into the Codex prompt in [backend/app/codex_service.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/codex_service.py#L72).

Is it essential? Depends on which part of the product you mean.
- Essential for the current “inspect” surface and impact analysis. Those features mostly stop making sense without the graph.
- Helpful but not strictly essential for code editing/building. The Codex path can continue without graph context; the code already falls back if graph context generation fails in [backend/app/codex_service.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/codex_service.py#L73).
- Not essential for unrelated surfaces like project profile, conversations, `AGENTS.md`, commit flow, or canvas CRUD.

If you removed it:
- You would lose indexing, structure tree, symbol search, relationship inspection, raw graph query, and impact analysis.
- Build quality would likely degrade because Codex would lose the “likely files / seed symbols” guidance, but builds could still run.
- The removal is not trivial, because the backend exposes graph endpoints as core API and the frontend initializes graph-backed views on load. This project is explicitly described as “code graph inspection and graph-backed code assistance” in [README.md](/home/wniak/konceptura/README.md#L3).

Practical bottom line: it is not essential for “an app that edits code at all,” but it is essential for Konceptura as currently designed. Removing it would turn the product from graph-assisted workspace tooling into a more generic repo chat/edit UI.
messages.title: In this codebase, code graph retrieval is a first-class feature, not just an optimization.

messages.content: interesting, do you think it's worth removing it? it's meant to be a visual vibecoding app thru specs, what do you think?

conversations.title: how does this project utilize code graph r...

messages.content:
Konceptura is a local, self-hosted workspace for understanding and changing a codebase with graph-assisted AI. In practice, it lets you point at a repository, index it into a Memgraph-backed code graph, browse the project structure and symbol relationships, run impact analysis from natural-language prompts, keep architecture notes on a canvas, and then ask Codex either to explain the repo, generate a plan, or make code changes through the backend. That overall shape is stated in [README.md](/home/wniak/konceptura/README.md) and wired through the FastAPI entry points in [backend/app/main.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/main.py).

The backend is the orchestration layer. [backend/app/services.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/services.py) handles code-graph queries, structure browsing, ranked symbol search, and impact analysis against Memgraph. [backend/app/codex_service.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/codex_service.py) wraps the local Codex CLI so the app can answer repo questions, produce implementation plans, apply real changes, and generate architecture notes. [backend/app/project_service.py](/home/wniak/konceptura/backend/app/project_service.py) adds project-level workflow features like repo selection, persistent conversation history in `.konceptura`, editable `AGENTS.md` instructions, and git commit creation/suggestion.

The frontend is a single workspace UI in [frontend/app/page.tsx](/home/wniak/konceptura/frontend/app/page.tsx). It exposes four main surfaces: notes, inspect, project, and chat. From there you can inspect the structure tree, search/query the graph, view impact results, edit the architecture canvas, manage repo context, review commit status, and trigger planning/build runs through the API client in [frontend/lib/api.ts](/home/wniak/konceptura/frontend/lib/api.ts).

So the short version is: this project is an AI-assisted local IDE/workbench for repo indexing, codebase inspection, architecture note-taking, and controlled code changes.
